Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

CTN 4, 111 [Lipšur litany][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
(start of obverse missing)
o? 1'o? 1'

[...] x x [...]

[...] ... [...];

o? 2'2'

[ina] ⸢GUNNI⸣ GI.⸢IZI⸣.[LA₂ ...]

[By] kindled brazier [...];

o? 3'3'

[ina mi]-ḫi-ir-ti x [...]

[In] front of ... [...]

o? 4'4'

[MUNUS.NU].⸢GIG?⸣ti te-še!(MU) ⸢še?-er?-ti?⸣ [...]

of the qadištu-priestess, [...] the morning's chaos;

o? 5'5'

⸢ZI?⸣ LUGAL* IDIM NUN u₃ MAŠ.GAG.<EN> ⸢il⸣-[ta-pat]

He has touched the throat of king, nobleman, prince or poor man;

o? 6'6'

gišPAN a-na pa-ni-šu iš-[ta-bir]

He has broken the bow at his disposal;

o? 7'7'

ku-pi-ra ik-ta-ba-[as]

He has trodden (on) bitumen;

o? 8'8'

gišx gišGAN.NA a-na pa-ni-šu₂ ⸢iš*?⸣-[...]

He ... [...] the ... (and) pestle at his disposal;

o? 9'9'

gišPAN u₃ nap-pa-ḫa-ti ⸢it?⸣-[ta-ma]

He has sworn (by) bow or bellows;

o? 10'10'

i-na mu-sa-ri u₃ me-dir-⸢ti⸣ [it-ta-ma]

[He has sworn] in garden or garden plot;

o? 11'11'

GI.⸢DUB?⸣.BA.A a-na pa-ni-šu₂ iš-x [...]

He ... [...] the stylus at his disposal;

o? 12'12'

GI.⸢DUB?⸣.BA.A ina ṣal-ti x [...]

... [...] the stylus in a quarrel;

o? 13'13'

⸢GI.DUB?⸣.BA.A la kit-ti ⸢it⸣-[...]

He ... [...] untruth (with) the stylus;

o? 14'14'

[...] ⸢DA?⸣ AR la kit-ti iš-⸢ta⸣-[...]

He ... [...] untruth [...] ...;

o? 15'15'

[...] x kit-ti i-te-pu-[uš]

He has done true [...] ...;

o? 16'16'

[...] x-ra?-ti it-ta-[...]

[...] ... [...];

o? 17'17'

[...] x GIŠ RA u₃ ma*?-ku-x [...]

[...] ... [...];

o? 18'18'

[...] x-la-a-ti it-x [...]

[...] ... [...];

o? 19'19'

[...]-la-a-ti x [...]

[...] ... [...];

(rest of obverse missing)
Reverse
r?r? NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r? 1'r? 1'

[...] x x x [...]

[...] ... [...];

r? 2'2'

x AN NI SAG x [...]

... [...];

r? 3'3'

⸢at⸣-ta MU ḪI ⸢GAL₂? SI?⸣ [...]

You ... [...];

r? 4'4'

⸢BU?⸣ IM? BA? it-ti A KUL? x [...]

... with ... [...];

r? 5'5'

[...] MU MA? [...]

[...] ... [...];

r? 6'6'

[...] x LI ME KA BU ša₂* ⸢i-na⸣ x [...]

[...] ... who in ... [...];

r? 7'7'

[...] x i-na šu*-bur-ri* [...]

[...] ... in the rump [...];

r? 8'8'

[...] x x BU UD x x x [...]

[...] ... [...];

r? 9'9'

[...]-a-aʾ gišBAN₂ gišBA.[...]

[...] ... sūtu of ... [...];

r? 10'10'

[...] ⸢NAB?⸣ TU BAD [...]

[...] ... [...];

r? 11'11'

[...] x A x dASAR*?.LU₂*?.ḪI [...]

[...] ... Asalluhi [...];

r? 12'12'

[...] x u₃ DINGIR-MEŠ? NA [...]

[...] and the gods ... [...];

r? 13'13'

[...] x x x EN x [...]

[...] ... [...].
